title: Gandhi Sarovar Project
summary: 200-acre development at Musi-Esa confluence with world's tallest Gandhi statue. ~500 families displaced without assessments.
whatHappened: The government announced a Rs 5,000 crore project at the Musi-Esa confluence featuring a 200-acre development and the world's tallest Gandhi statue. Approximately 500 families were displaced, including residents of Madhu Park Ridge Apartments, many of whom are retired defence personnel. Mahatma Gandhi's great-grandson Tushar Gandhi publicly opposed the project on February 22-23, 2026.
governmentResponse: The government described the project as a tribute to Mahatma Gandhi and a major tourism and development initiative for the city.
whatDataShows: No Social Impact Assessment, no Environmental Impact Assessment, and no public consultation were conducted before displacing families. Rs 5,000 crore allocated for this project compared to Rs 800 crore spent on Indiramma Indlu housing for the poor (14% of its Rs 5,800 crore allocation).

title: Lionel Messi Exhibition Match
summary: ~Rs 100 crore spent on a celebrity football match. Singareni Collieries used Rs 10 crore from CSR funds (Union Coal Ministry confirmed misuse).
whatHappened: An exhibition football match featuring Lionel Messi was held on December 13, 2025 at RGI Cricket Stadium. Singareni Collieries (SCCL) spent Rs 10 crore from CSR funds — Union Coal Ministry confirmed this was misuse. Additional ~Rs 70 crore in government funding alleged. Rs 5 crore spent on FIFA-standard turf at MCR HRD Centre for the CM's practice session. SCCL spent Rs 10 crore on the Messi event but only Rs 8 lakh on its own Formation Day (down from Rs 40-50 lakh under BRS). A viral video showed the CM pushing a teenager away from Messi while allowing his grandson selfie access.
governmentResponse: CM called it a 'completely private programme.' This was contradicted by the Deputy CM conducting official inspections of the venue.
whatDataShows: Rs 100 crore approximate total expenditure on a single exhibition match. SCCL Formation Day budget: Rs 8 lakh (down from Rs 40-50 lakh). Rs 5 crore on turf for CM's practice. CM's 'private programme' claim contradicted by Deputy CM's official site inspections.

title: Formula E vs Messi: Spending Contradiction
summary: Congress filed an ACB case against BRS's KTR for Rs 55 crore Formula E 'irregularities,' then spent ~Rs 100 crore on Messi match + ~Rs 200 crore on Miss World.
whatHappened: Congress filed an Anti-Corruption Bureau case against BRS leader KTR for alleged Rs 55 crore irregularities in the Formula E event. Subsequently, the same government spent an estimated Rs 100 crore on the Messi exhibition match and an alleged Rs 200 crore on hosting Miss World 2025.
governmentResponse: The government maintained the Formula E case was about corruption, not spending, and that the Messi event was privately funded.
whatDataShows: KTR's public response: 'Rs 46 crore for Formula-E was wrong... But Rs 200 crore for Miss World is right!' Formula E expenditure questioned: Rs 55 crore. Messi match + Miss World combined estimated expenditure: Rs 300 crore.

title: HYDRAA Demolitions
summary: HYDRAA demolished 262 buildings and reclaimed 111 acres by September 2024. High Court called it 'terrorising the people.' Selective enforcement alleged.
whatHappened: HYDRAA (Hyderabad Disaster Response and Asset Protection Agency) was formed in July 2024. By September 2024, it had demolished 262 buildings and reclaimed 111 acres. High Court Justice K. Lakshman stated HYDRAA was 'terrorising the people.' CM's brother Tirupati Reddy's house was allegedly spared. An orphanage for disabled children was demolished despite a court stay order. A woman in Kukatpally died by suicide on September 27, 2024, reportedly fearing demolition of her home.
governmentResponse: The government stated HYDRAA actions were necessary to protect lakes, water bodies, and government land from illegal encroachments.
whatDataShows: 262 buildings demolished, 111 acres reclaimed. Supreme Court issued demolition guidelines on November 13 and December 17, 2024 — in response to nationwide demolition concerns. High Court Justice K. Lakshman described HYDRAA as 'terrorising the people.' One suicide linked to demolition fear (September 27, 2024). Court stay order violated in orphanage demolition.

title: Musi Riverfront Demolitions
summary: 10,600 structures identified for demolition; 15,000+ households affected. 132+ houses demolished in first phase. No DPR, no R&R policy, no environmental clearances.
whatHappened: The Musi Riverfront Development project (estimated Rs 1.5 lakh crore) identified 10,600 structures for demolition affecting 15,000+ households. In the first phase on October 1-2, 2024, 132+ houses were demolished across Shankar Nagar, Vinayaknagar, and surrounding areas. Displaced families were predominantly from Muslim communities. 100+ homeowners obtained High Court stay orders against demolitions.
governmentResponse: The government described it as essential urban development and river rejuvenation, promising rehabilitation for displaced families.
whatDataShows: No Detailed Project Report has been published. No Rehabilitation & Resettlement policy exists. No environmental clearances obtained. Congress president Kharge advised the CM to show restraint. 100+ High Court stay orders obtained by homeowners. Disproportionate impact on Muslim families documented.

title: Telangana Thalli Statue Redesign
summary: State identity statue redesigned: traditional pink saree replaced with green saree and open palm resembling Congress symbol. Unveiled on Sonia Gandhi's birthday.
whatHappened: The Telangana Thalli (Mother Telangana) statue was unveiled on December 10, 2024 — Sonia Gandhi's birthday. The original design featured a pink saree, Bathukamma pot, and traditional crown. The new version features a green saree and an open palm gesture resembling the Congress party's election symbol. A Rajiv Gandhi statue was also installed opposite the Secretariat in September 2024.
governmentResponse: The government stated the new design represents the spirit of Telangana and that the colour choices had no political significance.
whatDataShows: Original statue: pink saree (Telangana's cultural colour), Bathukamma pot, traditional crown. New statue: green saree (Congress party colour), open palm (resembles Congress party symbol). Unveiled on Sonia Gandhi's birthday (December 10). Rajiv Gandhi statue installed opposite Secretariat.
